zh.Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle於1175年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特時，他的父親Robert de Harcourt23歲，母親Isabel de Camville24歲。他於1202年與Alice Noell在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1223年4月6日在他的家鄉享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle was born in 1175, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de Harcourt, was 23 and his mother, Isabel de Camville, was 24. He married Alice Noell in 1202, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 6 April 1223, in his hometown, at the age of 48.

Il a épousé Alice Noell en 1202, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1223, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castleが1175年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトで生まれた時，父Robert de Harcourtは23歳，母Isabel de Camvilleは24歳であった。1202年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでAlice Noellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1223年4月6日に故郷で48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle(이)가 1175년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Harcourt(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Camville(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1202년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Alice Noell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1223년 4월 6일 그의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle родился в 1175 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de Harcourt было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Camville, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Noell в 1202 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 6 апреля 1223 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 48 лет.
